id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0000
text:
![Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/media/noodzaak-voor-alternatieve-koeling_1191476566_tcm16-104578.jpg)
Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing. [Headlines & Insights](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/headlines-insights/index.html). [Technology, Media & Telecom](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/index.html). 24/03/2021 • 5 minutes read. [Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/julia-krauwer/index.html). Cooling installations at data centres in North Holland use so much water that this could locally lead to a drinking water shortage, De Telegraaf reported. Not every cooling system uses drinking water, though; there is an alternative, with many other benefits as well.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0003
text: Cooling installations at data centres in North Holland use so much water that this could locally lead to a drinking water shortage, De Telegraaf reported. Not every cooling system uses drinking water, though; there is an alternative, with many other benefits as well. Insight. Data centres’ drinking water use has its roots in so-called “open cooling towers”. Heat from IT equipment is absorbed by water, which is then sprayed in these cooling towers.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0003
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0004
text: Not every cooling system uses drinking water, though; there is an alternative, with many other benefits as well. Insight. Data centres’ drinking water use has its roots in so-called “open cooling towers”. Heat from IT equipment is absorbed by water, which is then sprayed in these cooling towers. Heat is thus automatically transferred to the outside air.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0004
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0005
text: Insight. Data centres’ drinking water use has its roots in so-called “open cooling towers”. Heat from IT equipment is absorbed by water, which is then sprayed in these cooling towers. Heat is thus automatically transferred to the outside air. Part of the water evaporates in this process and must be replenished.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0006
text: Data centres’ drinking water use has its roots in so-called “open cooling towers”. Heat from IT equipment is absorbed by water, which is then sprayed in these cooling towers. Heat is thus automatically transferred to the outside air. Part of the water evaporates in this process and must be replenished. A portion of the remaining water also has to be discharged regularly.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0007
text: Heat from IT equipment is absorbed by water, which is then sprayed in these cooling towers. Heat is thus automatically transferred to the outside air. Part of the water evaporates in this process and must be replenished. A portion of the remaining water also has to be discharged regularly. These systems are now under fire because they could cause a drinking water shortage on hot days.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0008
text: Heat is thus automatically transferred to the outside air. Part of the water evaporates in this process and must be replenished. A portion of the remaining water also has to be discharged regularly. These systems are now under fire because they could cause a drinking water shortage on hot days. The Dutch Data Center Association [plays down](https://fd.nl/futures/1377663/zorgen-om-onduidelijk-waterverbruik-datacenters-jvc1cawVIomh) in Het Financieele Dagblad the high water use that [De Telegraaf](https://www.telegraaf.nl/nieuws/1219494172/datacenters-slurpen-zo-veel-water-dat-er-mogelijk-te-weinig-voor-huishoudens-overblijft) mentions.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0009
text: Part of the water evaporates in this process and must be replenished. A portion of the remaining water also has to be discharged regularly. These systems are now under fire because they could cause a drinking water shortage on hot days. The Dutch Data Center Association [plays down](https://fd.nl/futures/1377663/zorgen-om-onduidelijk-waterverbruik-datacenters-jvc1cawVIomh) in Het Financieele Dagblad the high water use that [De Telegraaf](https://www.telegraaf.nl/nieuws/1219494172/datacenters-slurpen-zo-veel-water-dat-er-mogelijk-te-weinig-voor-huishoudens-overblijft) mentions. The FD article also states that this form of cooling is needed only on warmer days, about five per cent of the time.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0010
text: A portion of the remaining water also has to be discharged regularly. These systems are now under fire because they could cause a drinking water shortage on hot days. The Dutch Data Center Association [plays down](https://fd.nl/futures/1377663/zorgen-om-onduidelijk-waterverbruik-datacenters-jvc1cawVIomh) in Het Financieele Dagblad the high water use that [De Telegraaf](https://www.telegraaf.nl/nieuws/1219494172/datacenters-slurpen-zo-veel-water-dat-er-mogelijk-te-weinig-voor-huishoudens-overblijft) mentions. The FD article also states that this form of cooling is needed only on warmer days, about five per cent of the time. Even so, caretaker minister Van Nieuwenhuizen has said she wants to find out “[what is going on](https://www.rtlnieuws.nl/tech/artikel/5220867/datacentra-water-watergebruik-noord-holland-nederland-hollands-kroon)”.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0011
text: These systems are now under fire because they could cause a drinking water shortage on hot days. The Dutch Data Center Association [plays down](https://fd.nl/futures/1377663/zorgen-om-onduidelijk-waterverbruik-datacenters-jvc1cawVIomh) in Het Financieele Dagblad the high water use that [De Telegraaf](https://www.telegraaf.nl/nieuws/1219494172/datacenters-slurpen-zo-veel-water-dat-er-mogelijk-te-weinig-voor-huishoudens-overblijft) mentions. The FD article also states that this form of cooling is needed only on warmer days, about five per cent of the time. Even so, caretaker minister Van Nieuwenhuizen has said she wants to find out “[what is going on](https://www.rtlnieuws.nl/tech/artikel/5220867/datacentra-water-watergebruik-noord-holland-nederland-hollands-kroon)”. Whatever the case, to save drinking water data centres could consider a “closed cooling system”.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0012
text: The Dutch Data Center Association [plays down](https://fd.nl/futures/1377663/zorgen-om-onduidelijk-waterverbruik-datacenters-jvc1cawVIomh) in Het Financieele Dagblad the high water use that [De Telegraaf](https://www.telegraaf.nl/nieuws/1219494172/datacenters-slurpen-zo-veel-water-dat-er-mogelijk-te-weinig-voor-huishoudens-overblijft) mentions. The FD article also states that this form of cooling is needed only on warmer days, about five per cent of the time. Even so, caretaker minister Van Nieuwenhuizen has said she wants to find out “[what is going on](https://www.rtlnieuws.nl/tech/artikel/5220867/datacentra-water-watergebruik-noord-holland-nederland-hollands-kroon)”. Whatever the case, to save drinking water data centres could consider a “closed cooling system”. Unlike open systems like those described above, in a closed system the coolant keeps circulating and does not need to be replenished.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0013
text: The FD article also states that this form of cooling is needed only on warmer days, about five per cent of the time. Even so, caretaker minister Van Nieuwenhuizen has said she wants to find out “[what is going on](https://www.rtlnieuws.nl/tech/artikel/5220867/datacentra-water-watergebruik-noord-holland-nederland-hollands-kroon)”. Whatever the case, to save drinking water data centres could consider a “closed cooling system”. Unlike open systems like those described above, in a closed system the coolant keeps circulating and does not need to be replenished. It gets even more interesting when such a closed system is combined with efficient heat capture right at the source, the server.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0013
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0014
text: Even so, caretaker minister Van Nieuwenhuizen has said she wants to find out “[what is going on](https://www.rtlnieuws.nl/tech/artikel/5220867/datacentra-water-watergebruik-noord-holland-nederland-hollands-kroon)”. Whatever the case, to save drinking water data centres could consider a “closed cooling system”. Unlike open systems like those described above, in a closed system the coolant keeps circulating and does not need to be replenished. It gets even more interesting when such a closed system is combined with efficient heat capture right at the source, the server. This combination not only brings benefits in terms of water and energy use, but is also the most future-proof in view of the latest technological developments.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0015
text: Whatever the case, to save drinking water data centres could consider a “closed cooling system”. Unlike open systems like those described above, in a closed system the coolant keeps circulating and does not need to be replenished. It gets even more interesting when such a closed system is combined with efficient heat capture right at the source, the server. This combination not only brings benefits in terms of water and energy use, but is also the most future-proof in view of the latest technological developments. Server heat can be harvested via air or via liquid.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0015
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0016
text: Unlike open systems like those described above, in a closed system the coolant keeps circulating and does not need to be replenished. It gets even more interesting when such a closed system is combined with efficient heat capture right at the source, the server. This combination not only brings benefits in terms of water and energy use, but is also the most future-proof in view of the latest technological developments. Server heat can be harvested via air or via liquid. In the first case, cool air is blown past the server, the “server rack” or simply through the entire room.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0016
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0017
text: It gets even more interesting when such a closed system is combined with efficient heat capture right at the source, the server. This combination not only brings benefits in terms of water and energy use, but is also the most future-proof in view of the latest technological developments. Server heat can be harvested via air or via liquid. In the first case, cool air is blown past the server, the “server rack” or simply through the entire room. The heat is in turn removed in a warm airstream.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0017
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0018
text: This combination not only brings benefits in terms of water and energy use, but is also the most future-proof in view of the latest technological developments. Server heat can be harvested via air or via liquid. In the first case, cool air is blown past the server, the “server rack” or simply through the entire room. The heat is in turn removed in a warm airstream. Air is not a particularly good medium for transporting heat; liquid is a much better conductor.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0018
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0019
text: Server heat can be harvested via air or via liquid. In the first case, cool air is blown past the server, the “server rack” or simply through the entire room. The heat is in turn removed in a warm airstream. Air is not a particularly good medium for transporting heat; liquid is a much better conductor. Experts have therefore been predicting the breakthrough of liquid cooling for years, also known as “direct liquid cooling”.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0019
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0020
text: In the first case, cool air is blown past the server, the “server rack” or simply through the entire room. The heat is in turn removed in a warm airstream. Air is not a particularly good medium for transporting heat; liquid is a much better conductor. Experts have therefore been predicting the breakthrough of liquid cooling for years, also known as “direct liquid cooling”. Small tubes carrying coolant are led along or through the servers, or the entire server is placed in a bath of synthetic oil.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0020
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0021
text: The heat is in turn removed in a warm airstream. Air is not a particularly good medium for transporting heat; liquid is a much better conductor. Experts have therefore been predicting the breakthrough of liquid cooling for years, also known as “direct liquid cooling”. Small tubes carrying coolant are led along or through the servers, or the entire server is placed in a bath of synthetic oil. This latter, still new variant is also known as “immersion cooling”.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0021
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0022
text: Air is not a particularly good medium for transporting heat; liquid is a much better conductor. Experts have therefore been predicting the breakthrough of liquid cooling for years, also known as “direct liquid cooling”. Small tubes carrying coolant are led along or through the servers, or the entire server is placed in a bath of synthetic oil. This latter, still new variant is also known as “immersion cooling”. The data centre industry has not yet switched on a large scale, but the first tentative steps towards direct liquid cooling are now being taken.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0022
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0023
text: Experts have therefore been predicting the breakthrough of liquid cooling for years, also known as “direct liquid cooling”. Small tubes carrying coolant are led along or through the servers, or the entire server is placed in a bath of synthetic oil. This latter, still new variant is also known as “immersion cooling”. The data centre industry has not yet switched on a large scale, but the first tentative steps towards direct liquid cooling are now being taken. For example, Google cools part of its servers using small tubes directly on the [computer chips](https://datacenterfrontier.com/google-shifts-to-liquid-cooling-for-ai-data-crunching/).

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0023
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0024
text: Small tubes carrying coolant are led along or through the servers, or the entire server is placed in a bath of synthetic oil. This latter, still new variant is also known as “immersion cooling”. The data centre industry has not yet switched on a large scale, but the first tentative steps towards direct liquid cooling are now being taken. For example, Google cools part of its servers using small tubes directly on the [computer chips](https://datacenterfrontier.com/google-shifts-to-liquid-cooling-for-ai-data-crunching/). Microsoft has issued [encouraging reports](https://datacenterfrontier.com/microsoft-tests-immersion-cooling-tech-from-bitcoin-mining-for-its-cloud-servers/) on immersion cooling, and oil company [Shell](https://www.hpcwire.com/off-the-wire/asperitas-selected-by-shell-to-deliver-immersion-cooling-for-amsterdam-hpc-datacentre/) and French bank [Crédit Agricole](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/cr%C3%A9dit-agricole-opts-for-immersion-cooling/) have also said they will use this technology in their own data centres.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0024
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0025
text: This latter, still new variant is also known as “immersion cooling”. The data centre industry has not yet switched on a large scale, but the first tentative steps towards direct liquid cooling are now being taken. For example, Google cools part of its servers using small tubes directly on the [computer chips](https://datacenterfrontier.com/google-shifts-to-liquid-cooling-for-ai-data-crunching/). Microsoft has issued [encouraging reports](https://datacenterfrontier.com/microsoft-tests-immersion-cooling-tech-from-bitcoin-mining-for-its-cloud-servers/) on immersion cooling, and oil company [Shell](https://www.hpcwire.com/off-the-wire/asperitas-selected-by-shell-to-deliver-immersion-cooling-for-amsterdam-hpc-datacentre/) and French bank [Crédit Agricole](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/cr%C3%A9dit-agricole-opts-for-immersion-cooling/) have also said they will use this technology in their own data centres. Several considerations are at play here.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0026
text: The data centre industry has not yet switched on a large scale, but the first tentative steps towards direct liquid cooling are now being taken. For example, Google cools part of its servers using small tubes directly on the [computer chips](https://datacenterfrontier.com/google-shifts-to-liquid-cooling-for-ai-data-crunching/). Microsoft has issued [encouraging reports](https://datacenterfrontier.com/microsoft-tests-immersion-cooling-tech-from-bitcoin-mining-for-its-cloud-servers/) on immersion cooling, and oil company [Shell](https://www.hpcwire.com/off-the-wire/asperitas-selected-by-shell-to-deliver-immersion-cooling-for-amsterdam-hpc-datacentre/) and French bank [Crédit Agricole](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/cr%C3%A9dit-agricole-opts-for-immersion-cooling/) have also said they will use this technology in their own data centres. Several considerations are at play here. First, direct liquid cooling means lower energy use.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0026

metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0027
text: For example, Google cools part of its servers using small tubes directly on the [computer chips](https://datacenterfrontier.com/google-shifts-to-liquid-cooling-for-ai-data-crunching/). Microsoft has issued [encouraging reports](https://datacenterfrontier.com/microsoft-tests-immersion-cooling-tech-from-bitcoin-mining-for-its-cloud-servers/) on immersion cooling, and oil company [Shell](https://www.hpcwire.com/off-the-wire/asperitas-selected-by-shell-to-deliver-immersion-cooling-for-amsterdam-hpc-datacentre/) and French bank [Crédit Agricole](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/cr%C3%A9dit-agricole-opts-for-immersion-cooling/) have also said they will use this technology in their own data centres. Several considerations are at play here. First, direct liquid cooling means lower energy use. For immersion cooling this is even a [reduction](https://www.datacenterknowledge.com/power-and-cooling/liquid-cooling-can-lower-data-center-pue-s-not-its-main-draw) of 30 to 50 per cent.

metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0028
text: Microsoft has issued [encouraging reports](https://datacenterfrontier.com/microsoft-tests-immersion-cooling-tech-from-bitcoin-mining-for-its-cloud-servers/) on immersion cooling, and oil company [Shell](https://www.hpcwire.com/off-the-wire/asperitas-selected-by-shell-to-deliver-immersion-cooling-for-amsterdam-hpc-datacentre/) and French bank [Crédit Agricole](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/cr%C3%A9dit-agricole-opts-for-immersion-cooling/) have also said they will use this technology in their own data centres. Several considerations are at play here. First, direct liquid cooling means lower energy use. For immersion cooling this is even a [reduction](https://www.datacenterknowledge.com/power-and-cooling/liquid-cooling-can-lower-data-center-pue-s-not-its-main-draw) of 30 to 50 per cent. Because cooling takes place directly at the source, the liquid — oil, in this case — reaches temperatures of 50 to 60 degrees Celsius.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0028
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0029
text: Several considerations are at play here. First, direct liquid cooling means lower energy use. For immersion cooling this is even a [reduction](https://www.datacenterknowledge.com/power-and-cooling/liquid-cooling-can-lower-data-center-pue-s-not-its-main-draw) of 30 to 50 per cent. Because cooling takes place directly at the source, the liquid — oil, in this case — reaches temperatures of 50 to 60 degrees Celsius. And counter-intuitive as it may sound, waste heat at a higher temperature cools down more easily — a principle of physics.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0029
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0030
text: First, direct liquid cooling means lower energy use. For immersion cooling this is even a [reduction](https://www.datacenterknowledge.com/power-and-cooling/liquid-cooling-can-lower-data-center-pue-s-not-its-main-draw) of 30 to 50 per cent. Because cooling takes place directly at the source, the liquid — oil, in this case — reaches temperatures of 50 to 60 degrees Celsius. And counter-intuitive as it may sound, waste heat at a higher temperature cools down more easily — a principle of physics. Heat removal that initially takes place via air later in the process yields water with residual heat temperatures of only 25 to 30 degrees, which conversely requires more intervention to cool.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0030
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0031
text: For immersion cooling this is even a [reduction](https://www.datacenterknowledge.com/power-and-cooling/liquid-cooling-can-lower-data-center-pue-s-not-its-main-draw) of 30 to 50 per cent. Because cooling takes place directly at the source, the liquid — oil, in this case — reaches temperatures of 50 to 60 degrees Celsius. And counter-intuitive as it may sound, waste heat at a higher temperature cools down more easily — a principle of physics. Heat removal that initially takes place via air later in the process yields water with residual heat temperatures of only 25 to 30 degrees, which conversely requires more intervention to cool. Think of fans that blow cool air along the warmed water.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0031
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0032
text: Because cooling takes place directly at the source, the liquid — oil, in this case — reaches temperatures of 50 to 60 degrees Celsius. And counter-intuitive as it may sound, waste heat at a higher temperature cools down more easily — a principle of physics. Heat removal that initially takes place via air later in the process yields water with residual heat temperatures of only 25 to 30 degrees, which conversely requires more intervention to cool. Think of fans that blow cool air along the warmed water. At higher outside temperatures it even involves “chillers”, cooling machines that use even more energy.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0032
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0033
text: And counter-intuitive as it may sound, waste heat at a higher temperature cools down more easily — a principle of physics. Heat removal that initially takes place via air later in the process yields water with residual heat temperatures of only 25 to 30 degrees, which conversely requires more intervention to cool. Think of fans that blow cool air along the warmed water. At higher outside temperatures it even involves “chillers”, cooling machines that use even more energy. An even more important consideration for switching to liquid cooling is the large amount of heat generated by the latest generation of computer chips.

Think of fans that blow cool air along the warmed water. At higher outside temperatures it even involves “chillers”, cooling machines that use even more energy. An even more important consideration for switching to liquid cooling is the large amount of heat generated by the latest generation of computer chips. These chips are specially developed for applications that require a lot of computing power, such as the development of artificial intelligence.

text: An even more important consideration for switching to liquid cooling is the large amount of heat generated by the latest generation of computer chips. These chips are specially developed for applications that require a lot of computing power, such as the development of artificial intelligence. Even the most advanced air cooling is, according to experts, unable to eliminate the heat released in the process. Only liquid cooling seems up to the task. This form of cooling also takes up less space, an advantage given the expected increase in the number of data centres.

text: These chips are specially developed for applications that require a lot of computing power, such as the development of artificial intelligence. Even the most advanced air cooling is, according to experts, unable to eliminate the heat released in the process. Only liquid cooling seems up to the task. This form of cooling also takes up less space, an advantage given the expected increase in the number of data centres. With the roll-out of 5G, more data centres will also appear in urban environments close to the end user.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0039
text: Even the most advanced air cooling is, according to experts, unable to eliminate the heat released in the process. Only liquid cooling seems up to the task. This form of cooling also takes up less space, an advantage given the expected increase in the number of data centres. With the roll-out of 5G, more data centres will also appear in urban environments close to the end user. These are smaller facilities that support all kinds of smart applications based on artificial intelligence.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0039

metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0040
text: Only liquid cooling seems up to the task. This form of cooling also takes up less space, an advantage given the expected increase in the number of data centres. With the roll-out of 5G, more data centres will also appear in urban environments close to the end user. These are smaller facilities that support all kinds of smart applications based on artificial intelligence. Liquid cooling is ideal in such cases, not only because of the advanced, heat-generating chips used, but also because this method can limit the number of square metres of data floor.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0040
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0041
text: This form of cooling also takes up less space, an advantage given the expected increase in the number of data centres. With the roll-out of 5G, more data centres will also appear in urban environments close to the end user. These are smaller facilities that support all kinds of smart applications based on artificial intelligence. Liquid cooling is ideal in such cases, not only because of the advanced, heat-generating chips used, but also because this method can limit the number of square metres of data floor. Air cooling often requires high spaces, large installations and wide aisles on either side of the servers.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0042
text: With the roll-out of 5G, more data centres will also appear in urban environments close to the end user. These are smaller facilities that support all kinds of smart applications based on artificial intelligence. Liquid cooling is ideal in such cases, not only because of the advanced, heat-generating chips used, but also because this method can limit the number of square metres of data floor. Air cooling often requires high spaces, large installations and wide aisles on either side of the servers. An alternative way of cooling also better meets the increasing societal demands placed on data centres.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0042

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0043
text: These are smaller facilities that support all kinds of smart applications based on artificial intelligence. Liquid cooling is ideal in such cases, not only because of the advanced, heat-generating chips used, but also because this method can limit the number of square metres of data floor. Air cooling often requires high spaces, large installations and wide aisles on either side of the servers. An alternative way of cooling also better meets the increasing societal demands placed on data centres. Last year Amsterdam stated that it would allow the construction of new data centres only if they [make their waste heat available free of charge](https://www.parool.nl/amsterdam/amsterdamse-datacenters-moeten-woningen-verwarmen-gratis~be0be8f4/) for heating homes in Greater Amsterdam.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0043

metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0044
text: Liquid cooling is ideal in such cases, not only because of the advanced, heat-generating chips used, but also because this method can limit the number of square metres of data floor. Air cooling often requires high spaces, large installations and wide aisles on either side of the servers. An alternative way of cooling also better meets the increasing societal demands placed on data centres. Last year Amsterdam stated that it would allow the construction of new data centres only if they [make their waste heat available free of charge](https://www.parool.nl/amsterdam/amsterdamse-datacenters-moeten-woningen-verwarmen-gratis~be0be8f4/) for heating homes in Greater Amsterdam. The high residual heat temperatures harvested via direct liquid cooling are widely applicable.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0044
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0045
text: Air cooling often requires high spaces, large installations and wide aisles on either side of the servers. An alternative way of cooling also better meets the increasing societal demands placed on data centres. Last year Amsterdam stated that it would allow the construction of new data centres only if they [make their waste heat available free of charge](https://www.parool.nl/amsterdam/amsterdamse-datacenters-moeten-woningen-verwarmen-gratis~be0be8f4/) for heating homes in Greater Amsterdam. The high residual heat temperatures harvested via direct liquid cooling are widely applicable. Waste heat at a relatively low temperature is less easy to use, because it can heat only well-insulated new-build homes.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0045
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0046
text: An alternative way of cooling also better meets the increasing societal demands placed on data centres. Last year Amsterdam stated that it would allow the construction of new data centres only if they [make their waste heat available free of charge](https://www.parool.nl/amsterdam/amsterdamse-datacenters-moeten-woningen-verwarmen-gratis~be0be8f4/) for heating homes in Greater Amsterdam. The high residual heat temperatures harvested via direct liquid cooling are widely applicable. Waste heat at a relatively low temperature is less easy to use, because it can heat only well-insulated new-build homes. Of course, this does not mean data centres will switch cooling techniques overnight.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0046
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0047
text: Last year Amsterdam stated that it would allow the construction of new data centres only if they [make their waste heat available free of charge](https://www.parool.nl/amsterdam/amsterdamse-datacenters-moeten-woningen-verwarmen-gratis~be0be8f4/) for heating homes in Greater Amsterdam. The high residual heat temperatures harvested via direct liquid cooling are widely applicable. Waste heat at a relatively low temperature is less easy to use, because it can heat only well-insulated new-build homes. Of course, this does not mean data centres will switch cooling techniques overnight. Liquid-based cooling systems involve a paradigm shift: they require differently designed buildings, adjustments and standardisation of the servers themselves, and a change in operational processes.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0047
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0048
text: The high residual heat temperatures harvested via direct liquid cooling are widely applicable. Waste heat at a relatively low temperature is less easy to use, because it can heat only well-insulated new-build homes. Of course, this does not mean data centres will switch cooling techniques overnight. Liquid-based cooling systems involve a paradigm shift: they require differently designed buildings, adjustments and standardisation of the servers themselves, and a change in operational processes. This entails significant investment.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0048
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0049
text: Waste heat at a relatively low temperature is less easy to use, because it can heat only well-insulated new-build homes. Of course, this does not mean data centres will switch cooling techniques overnight. Liquid-based cooling systems involve a paradigm shift: they require differently designed buildings, adjustments and standardisation of the servers themselves, and a change in operational processes. This entails significant investment. Yet the benefits do seem to be recognised by data centre owners; in recent research 60 per cent of respondents said they would consider liquid cooling.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0049
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0050
text: Of course, this does not mean data centres will switch cooling techniques overnight. Liquid-based cooling systems involve a paradigm shift: they require differently designed buildings, adjustments and standardisation of the servers themselves, and a change in operational processes. This entails significant investment. Yet the benefits do seem to be recognised by data centre owners; in recent research 60 per cent of respondents said they would consider liquid cooling. The percentage was even higher (70 per cent) when this question was asked specifically on the assumption that they would have to [build](https://www.delltechnologies.com/resources/en-us/asset/white-papers/products/ready-solutions/informa-liquid-cooling-survey-results.pdf) a new facility.

metadata.chunk_id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0050
metadata.source_slug: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe
metadata.source_title: Need for alternative cooling in data centres is increasing

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0051
text: Liquid-based cooling systems involve a paradigm shift: they require differently designed buildings, adjustments and standardisation of the servers themselves, and a change in operational processes. This entails significant investment. Yet the benefits do seem to be recognised by data centre owners; in recent research 60 per cent of respondents said they would consider liquid cooling. The percentage was even higher (70 per cent) when this question was asked specifically on the assumption that they would have to [build](https://www.delltechnologies.com/resources/en-us/asset/white-papers/products/ready-solutions/informa-liquid-cooling-survey-results.pdf) a new facility. All in all, it seems the ground for liquid cooling in data centres is becoming ever more fertile.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0052
text: This entails significant investment. Yet the benefits do seem to be recognised by data centre owners; in recent research 60 per cent of respondents said they would consider liquid cooling. The percentage was even higher (70 per cent) when this question was asked specifically on the assumption that they would have to [build](https://www.delltechnologies.com/resources/en-us/asset/white-papers/products/ready-solutions/informa-liquid-cooling-survey-results.pdf) a new facility. All in all, it seems the ground for liquid cooling in data centres is becoming ever more fertile. A growing focus on sustainability in society is a key driver, as are the demands that new digital applications will place on our digital infrastructure in the coming years.

id: Noodzaak voor alternatieve koeling datacenters neemt toe__c5-sentence-window__0053
text:
Yet the benefits do seem to be recognised by data centre owners; in recent research 60 per cent of respondents said they would consider liquid cooling. The percentage was even higher (70 per cent) when this question was asked specifically on the assumption that they would have to [build](https://www.delltechnologies.com/resources/en-us/asset/white-papers/products/ready-solutions/informa-liquid-cooling-survey-results.pdf) a new facility. All in all, it seems the ground for liquid cooling in data centres is becoming ever more fertile. A growing focus on sustainability in society is a key driver, as are the demands that new digital applications will place on our digital infrastructure in the coming years. It is now up to all forward-looking entrepreneurs to decide when they will place this final piece of the puzzle. *In Headlines & Insights, ABN AMRO interpret the news.*
